title: Linear search — stop at the first match
objectives: Follow a cursor through a collection until a condition matches
Explain why an early return stops the loop and the function
Transfer the structure to renamed values and a different condition

blocks.text: How can a function stop as soon as it finds the first matching item?

blocks.content: Goal: connect the current item, comparison, chosen branch, and returned result.

blocks.prompt: The cursor reaches 4 and the condition 4 > 0 is true. What happens next?

steps: Select the next item from the collection.
Compare the current item with the search condition.
Follow the branch recorded by execution.
Return immediately when the condition matches.
Return the fallback only when no item matches.

questions.prompt: What makes the renamed example a linear search?
questions.rubric: It returns as soon as the condition matches.

fields.value: Cursor plus comparison inside a loop
Returned only after the loop finds no match
Change the condition and input values without changing the structure
